description: A signed leaderboard score. Users play locally, call /api/sign-score on the site which validates the run and returns a server HMAC, then createRecord on their own PDS. Records without a valid server signature are ignored when rendering the leaderboard.

game.description: The game identifier. Includes the variant for games with multiple boards (e.g. typing-30).

score.description: The numeric score. Meaning depends on the game — for snake, food eaten; for typing, wpm; for wordle, guesses used (lower is better).

subject.description: AT-URI of the leaderboard marker record this score is claiming against.

did.description: The author's DID, copied into the record so the server HMAC binds the score to the player. Must match the record repo's DID when displayed.

achievedAt.description: Timestamp assigned by the signing server, part of the HMAC payload.

sig.description: Base64url-encoded HMAC-SHA256 of the canonical {game, score, did, achievedAt} payload, signed with a server secret. The leaderboard renderer verifies this and drops records that don't match.

replay.description: Optional compact replay data (input log, guess sequence, typed text hash). Not required; when present, the signing server may use it for additional validation before signing.
